heading: Patent, plant variety protection, and copyright cases
label: 1454 Patent, plant variety protection, and copyright cases
notes: Effective DateSection applicable to any civil action commenced on or after , see , set out as an Effective Date of 2011 Amendment note under .Sept. 16, 2011section 19(e) of Pub. L. 112–29section 1295 of this title
amendments: Effective DateSection applicable to any civil action commenced on or after , see , set out as an Effective Date of 2011 Amendment note under .Sept. 16, 2011section 19(e) of Pub. L. 112–29section 1295 of this title

children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: A civil action in which any party asserts a claim for relief arising under any Act of Congress relating to patents, plant variety protection, or copyrights may be removed to the district court of the United States for the district and division embracing the place where the action is pending.
the action may be removed by any party; and
the time limitations contained in section 1446(b) may be extended at any time for cause shown.

children.heading: .—Clarification of Jurisdiction in Certain Cases
children.label: c .—Clarification of Jurisdiction in Certain Cases
children.text: The court to which a civil action is removed under this section is not precluded from hearing and determining any claim in the civil action because the State court from which the civil action is removed did not have jurisdiction over that claim.
shall remand all claims that are neither a basis for removal under subsection (a) nor within the original or supplemental jurisdiction of the district court under any Act of Congress; and
may, under the circumstances specified in section 1367(c), remand any claims within the supplemental jurisdiction of the district court under section 1367.
